pages.raw_text:
Omitted Press Association.)


Attorney Kizer, representing the
National Surety Co., today gave no
tice of appeal from Judge Warrens
decision yesterday in favor of the
county commissioners. The surety
company asked for a permanent In
junction against the payment Of
$4,000 to Attorney III. Nj. Stephens
as the countys share in the expense
of the railroad rate case brought by
the city against the Northern Pa
cific and other lines.


The superior court ruled that the
commissioners had right to divert
public funds to this purpose. The
surety company maintains that the
appropriation is illegal. The COM
missioners acted on the advice OF
Prosecuting Attorney Barnhart, who
represented Stephens in the effort
to Collect the money.
